name: Rhys Shaw
summary: Final-year Astrophysics PhD Student at the University of Bristol, with expert skills in Machine Learning, Data Analysis, and Programming.

work_experience.description: Engineered an internal LLM client tool to interact with various LLM APIs while ensuring local data storage and local network document integration. Developed a full-stack web application using FastAPI and HTMX for the front and backend, with data managed in MongoDB.

Provided lab demonstrations and technical guidance to second-year undergraduates completing research projects. Leveraged detailed knowledge of physics experiments to evaluate student progress through interviews and the marking of technical reports.

education.thesis: Machine Learning and Analysis Techniques for Extra-Galactic Astronomy.
The Effect of Fractal Substructure in Early Stellar Cluster Evolution.
Exploring the bias in X-ray and SZ selected galaxy clusters using scaling relations.

experience.title: DataAid Committee Member
experience.description: Committee member for a student-led hackathon providing data science support to non-profits. Responsible for event organization, technical feasibility assessment of charity projects, and providing on-site technical support to participants.

experience.title: Astrophysics Developer Group co-Lead
experience.description: Co-lead of an open group for PhD students and academics focused on research software development. Facilitate weekly discussions on coding challenges and deliver tutorials on topics including parallelization, self-hosting, Git, and Docker.

awards.name: AIMLAC CDT PhD Studentship
awards.description: Awarded a fully funded PhD studentship from the AIMLAC CDT at the University of Bristol.

awards.name: BSc Research Project Commendation
awards.description: Commendation for research exploring the bias in X-ray and SZ selected galaxy clusters using scaling relations.

awards.name: Fundamentals of Accelerated Computing with Modern CUDA C++
The Complete Web Developer in 2022: Zero to Mastery

projects.name: STILTS-NLI
projects.description: A natural language interface for the astrophysics software STILTS, featuring a CLI that utilizes a fine-tuned, open-source language model.

projects.name: DRUID
projects.description: A Python package developed for my PhD that performs source finding on astronomical images using persistent homology.

projects.name: Personal Website
projects.description: A self-hosted portfolio site built using FastAPI and HTMX.

projects.name: Home-Lab
projects.description: A self-hosted server environment running various services, including my personal website, cloud photo storage and a Zotero cloud.
